summary: Rule chunk from The Rules page 62, chunk 2.

mechanics.raw_text:
Tension dissipates when you let players spend long minutes contemplating positions, carefully considering tactics and ap- plicable rules, and debating the next move, That is time-consuming, boring, and ‘unrealistic, n a real combat situation, you're lucky if you have enough time to be afraid, let, ‘lone time to study your next move. WH Don't Give Them Time to Think! Set the scene with a minimum of etal tll ing only what Is absolutely necessary to the 
IM: You enter the room. There are two desks ‘and one other door. Two traitors leap up from behind the desks and spray you with bullets. What do you do? Dont let a player ruminate. Each combat round represents five seconds, and there's not much time for ruminating in five seconds, If player hesitates, his character hesitates. Ifa player says, “Wait, I'm thinking.” thats just ‘what his character is doing, too... while the bad guys pepper him with slugs. Quick think: ing is what counts in Paranoia combat 
I Reward Flamboyance and Wit 
Paranoia should have the feel of a movie, not a wargame. Paranoia combat shouldet be realistic — it should be dramatic, like movie combat. Exceptionally heroic, cowardly, bizarre, or ektravagant actions should generally succeed (though probably not the way the player expected them to — a regular perverse twist isan effective deterrent t player abuse) 
Wh Kill the Bastards! Don't be afraid to kill characters in Paranoia ‘The world of Paranoia is unimaginably dead- ly, teeming with traitors, enemies, lunatics defective devices, lethal environmental hhazards, and your best buddy The Computer, fa character gets dead, let him be dead — there are no last-minute saves, no divine in.
